evidence_quotes.quote: when you BUY a video game nowadays, the company publishing said video game gives a LICENSE to play YOUR game.
You paid for it but you don't own it.

paragraphs.text: Hello, I'm not a lawyer but if I understood correctly the DFA tries to prevents EU citizens from being scammed but it seems that should also include video games. Why? It's simple: when you BUY a video game nowadays, the company publishing said video game gives a LICENSE to play YOUR game. You paid for it but you don't own it. So, when (not if) said publisher erase the game from their servers, you are left with NOTHING! No game. No refund. There are HUNDREDS of games who are already DEAD and became lost media and there are HUNDREDS more to follow! Look it up. Thanks in advance.
